card.intuition: For each bitmask, compute the sum of f[sub] over all submasks sub of mask. Naïve enumeration is O(3ⁿ). SOS DP achieves O(n · 2ⁿ) by processing one bit at a time: dp[mask] = dp[mask with bit i cleared] + dp[mask] for each bit i.
card.analogy: Calculating the total votes a candidate received from any subset of districts: instead of re-counting every possible coalition, you add up contributions bit-by-bit — each pass considers one more district as potentially included.
when_to_use: Sum / min / max over all subsets of a mask
Counting pairs (A, B) where A is a submask of B
gotchas: Process each bit i independently: for all masks where bit i is set, dp[mask] += dp[mask ^ (1 << i)]
SOS computes the sum of f over ALL submasks — not just immediate sub-masks
Superset sum DP is the reverse: for all masks where bit i is NOT set, dp[mask] += dp[mask | (1 << i)]

exercise.prompt: Implement SOS DP: for each mask, compute the sum of f[sub] over all submasks sub.
exercise.starter_code:
def sos_dp(f, n):
    """
    f : list of length 2^n (indexed by bitmask)
    n : number of bits
    Returns dp where dp[mask] = sum of f[sub] for all sub that are submasks of mask.
    """
    dp = f[:]
    for i in range(n):              # process bit i
        for mask in range(1 << n):
            if mask >> i & 1:       # bit i is set in mask
                dp[mask] += ___________  # add contribution of sub-mask with bit i cleared
    return dp
hints: mask ^ (1 << i) clears bit i from mask — this gives the submask that doesn't include element i. Add its dp value to propagate subset sums upward.
